growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (routine liquid culture); biofilm/co-culture incubations static in microtiter plates/tubes
growth_records.medium: Standard Marine Media (SMM), artificial seawater medium supplemented with yeast extract and tryptone
growth_records.cite: Basso et al. Genetically similar temperate phages form coalitions with their shared host that lead to niche-specific fitness effects. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0637-z. [PMC7305329]
growth_records.snippet: Doubling times of CB-D and CB-A in liquid culture are 75 and 100 min, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported directly as 75 min; converted to hours (75/60=1.25 h) then mu=ln2/1.25=0.5545 h^-1. Grown routinely at 25°C in the dark, 200 rpm shaking, in SMM (artificial seawater + 0.11% yeast extract + 0.2% tryptone); aerobic condition inferred from shaking liquid culture (not explicitly labeled 'aerobic' in text).

growth_records.strain: CB-A (phi-A superinfected double lysogen derivative of CB-D)
growth_records.agit: 200 rpm (routine liquid culture); biofilm/co-culture incubations static in microtiter plates/tubes
growth_records.medium: Standard Marine Media (SMM), artificial seawater medium supplemented with yeast extract and tryptone
growth_records.cite: Basso et al. Genetically similar temperate phages form coalitions with their shared host that lead to niche-specific fitness effects. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0637-z. [PMC7305329]
growth_records.snippet: Doubling times of CB-D and CB-A in liquid culture are 75 and 100 min, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time reported directly as 100 min; converted to hours (100/60=1.6667 h) then mu=ln2/1.6667=0.4159 h^-1. Same medium/conditions as CB-D (25°C, dark, 200 rpm, SMM); CB-A grows slower and reaches lower stationary-phase density than CB-D per the paper.

growth_records.medium: F/2 (instant ocean) seawater medium, co-culture with Phaeodactylum tricornutum, no added organic matter
growth_records.cite: Gilbert, Naomi E. et al. A bloom of a single bacterium shapes the microbiome during outdoor diatom cultivation collapse. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00375-25. [PMC12172471]
growth_records.snippet: This isolate also grows in co-culture (growth rate of 0.35 day-1) with P. tricornutum (growth rate of 0.35 day-1) at perpetuity with no added organic matter (Fig. S11).
growth_records.notes: Only explicit numeric prokaryote growth rate in the paper. Reported mu = 0.35 day-1 for Sulfitobacter sp. N5S in perpetual co-culture with P. tricornutum (no added organic matter). Converted to per hour: 0.35 / 24 = 0.01458 ~ 0.0146 h-1. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.35 = 1.98 days = 1.98 x 24 = 47.5 h. Temperature 22C taken from the study's laboratory cultivation standard (lysate/co-culture experiments at 22C); pH not stated for this co-culture. Medium is F/2 (instant ocean) seawater used for P. tricornutum; full composition not given -> medium_composition_stated:false. N5S isolated from Bodega Bay, CA; genome NCBI PRJNA581036. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates reported (paper is metagenomics/metaproteomics of algal-pond microbiome; OD600 monitored in lysate cross-feeding assay but no numeric rates). Strain OT-1 = Kordia algicida OT-1 (NBRC 100033) cultivated but no numeric growth/uptake rate reported.
